current_implemented_build: V60.3.13 Stoplight Trust Color and PNG Path Recovery
patch_type: Stoplight trust-state color correction after V60.3.12 PNG asset integration
release_chain_status: V60.3.13 is the current implemented build. It loads after V60.3.12, enforces stoplight green/yellow/red for TrustMap hover rings, selected rings, status dots, selected asset score rings, selected asset score accents, and retries likely uploaded PNG filename variants. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy and after the PNG assets are uploaded to assets/trustmap/v60-3-12/.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V60.3.13. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
v60_3_13_scope_rule: V60.3.13 corrects trust-state color logic after V60.3.12. The hologram PNG art keeps its native color, but hover rings, selected rings, status dots, selected asset score rings, selected asset score accents, and trust-state indicators use stoplight green, yellow, or red.

stoplight_trust_color_rule.principle: Trust-state colors are stoplight green, yellow, and red only. Do not use neon blue as the default hover or trust-state color when an object has a trust state. Neon blue may remain a neutral brand/structure color, but trust-state affordances must use stoplight color.

connector_limitation: The GitHub text connector can create and update UTF-8 text files but cannot directly upload binary PNG files. The generated cybershield_v60_3_12_trustmap_png_assets.zip must be manually uploaded/extracted into assets/trustmap/v60-3-12/, or the PNG files must be uploaded manually through GitHub. If the PNG files are not physically present in the repo path, GitHub Pages cannot render them regardless of JS mapping logic.
non_negotiable_trustmap_scope: Do not replace the TrustMap page
Do not remove the left Operational Trust Score panel
Do not remove the right-side score area
Do not remove Layer 2 or Layer 3
Do not replace the radar / constellation environment
Do not override #v554World transform with CSS
Do not create a second movement system
Only map rendered PNG assets into the existing TrustMap core and Layer 1 slots
Trust-state colors must be stoplight green, yellow, or red only

public_trustmap_language.title: Enterprise Trust Map
public_trustmap_language.subtitle: Decision Trust Universe
public_trustmap_language.rule: Do not expose builder language such as registry-driven, renders from registry, versioned render source, implementation layer, or internal version source in executive-facing TrustMap UI.

trustmap_visual_doctrine: The original TrustMap shell is preserved. Rendered PNG assets own the core and Layer 1 visuals. The base V55.4 renderer owns movement and detail modes. V60.3.12 maps assets into existing slots and adds sliders that simulate the same base drag/wheel interactions. V60.3.13 applies stoplight trust-state colors over those assets and score states.
selected_asset_panel_rule: The selected-asset panel belongs below the right-side selected asset score. It changes when the user hovers or focuses a Layer 1 graphical asset. It must not duplicate the base Layer 1 Domain Detail panel.
layer1_hover_focus_selected_rule: Hovering, focusing, or selecting any Layer 1 PNG asset must show a neon circular selection ring around that asset. Clicking a Layer 1 asset keeps the ring active until another Layer 1 asset is selected. The right panel must match the selected Layer 1 asset. The ring color must be stoplight green, yellow, or red based on that asset's trust state.
score_rule: Left score is the Operational Trust Score for the current scenario/environment. Right score is the Selected Asset Trust Score for the hovered/focused Layer 1 asset. Those scores must not be duplicated unless they intentionally represent the same metric. Selected asset score color must match the asset's stoplight trust state.
information_architecture_rule: Evidence tab owns the full Manual Evidence Workbench. Runtime tab owns compact evidence requirements for action. Proof Pack tab owns proof-oriented evidence trace and defensibility context. Settings owns build/version/governance/prototype metadata.
controlling_doctrine: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
model_maturity_rule: CyberShield scoring models are Expert-Derived V1 Trust Scoring Models, not Statistically Validated Trust Models. Default weights are calibratable and not authoritative until validated through test scenarios, expert review, red-team attempts, and real-world use cases.
v60_3_13_changes: enforces stoplight green/yellow/red for trust-state visual indicators
keeps native hologram PNG art colors intact
applies stoplight color to Layer 1 hover rings
applies stoplight color to Layer 1 selected rings
applies stoplight color to Layer 1 status dots
applies stoplight color to the right Selected Asset Trust Score ring
applies stoplight color to selected asset score accents
retries likely PNG filename variants when the initial normalized path fails
preserves V60.3.12 movement recovery and slider behavior

trustmap_png_asset_integration.boundary: Static advisory visual integration and interaction recovery only. No live scoring, evidence retrieval, workflow automation, ticketing, notifications, backend persistence, enforcement, statistical validation, certification, compliance validation, or live integrations.

stoplight_trust_color_and_png_path_recov.boundary: Static visual trust-state correction and filename fallback recovery only. No live scoring or evidence retrieval.

recommended_next_build.label: V60.3.14 Global Stoplight Trust State and MJC Light Background Pass
goals: apply stoplight green/yellow/red hover/focus/selected trust-state affordances across CyberShield, not only TrustMap
replace confusing default neon-blue hover highlights for trust-state objects
keep neon blue for neutral brand/structure lines only where no trust state is implied
lighten the main body background from midnight/command-center blue toward the bright white-blue MJC shield teleportal glow
keep the Operational Trust Control Plane black command-center style where appropriate
do not change TrustMap PNG asset wiring until PNG files are physically present in the repo path

lessons_learned: Do not ask the assistant to invent production-grade visual art when high-fidelity branded graphics are required.
Use a specialist graphic artist or image generation workflow for rendered hologram assets.
Use the assistant for integration, layout, interaction recovery, repo hygiene, source-of-truth documentation, and QA discipline.
The base TrustMap renderer must own movement.
Rendered PNG assets must own visuals.
A V60 layer should map assets into existing slots, not fight the base renderer with transform overrides.
When a connector cannot upload binary assets, package the assets and document the exact manual upload path rather than pretending the assets are operational.
Trust-state color and native visual asset color are different concepts. Trust-state means stoplight green, yellow, or red.
prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live URL ingestion, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, backend persistence, statistical validation, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: PNG assets must be uploaded to assets/trustmap/v60-3-12/
If PNG files are absent, GitHub Pages cannot render them
Models are expert-derived V1 and not statistically validated
No live scoring, evidence retrieval, ticketing, notifications, or workflow automation
